rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM SAYSON YAO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/19/2021. 1) Notice of the petition was given on 03/02/2021 prior to the petition being filed on 03/03/2021. Notice must be given after the petition has been filed. Matter continued to provide proper notice. [See DE-120 page 1 #1.] 2) Unless otherwise permitted for good cause, if an account exceeds two years, the first two years must be filed separately. After the first two-year account is adjudicated, subsequent account periods may not be for more than three (3) years and each three year period must be filed separately. [LRSF 14.35] 3) Petitioner was bonded for $300,000.00. On 06/17/2020 when Petitioner filed the I&A indicating bond was insufficient but did not file an ex parte for an increase in bond. [LRSF 14.11] See CRC 7.204. When the bond of a fiduciary must be increased, the Court favors filing of an additional bond rather than a substitute bond. An account will not be approved until the additional bond is on file. [LRSF 14.16] 4) Please explain why $345,297.18 is in a Fidelity Brokerage account when the FDIC limit is $250,000.00. 5) If a beneficiary satisfies the requirement of Probate Code 11801, a certified copy of Letters certified within sixty (60) days and issued to the personal representative of the estate of the beneficiary, the original 13100 affidavit executed by the person(s) entitled to the beneficiary's share, or a certified copy of the Spousal/Domestic Partner Property Order issued to the beneficiary's surviving spouse/registered domestic partner must be filed before the hearing date. [LRSF 14.36 G.] 6) Please file a memorandum of points and authorities in support of the San Francisco Probate court ordering distribution according to Filipino Probate Law and Philippines Probate Code. 7) Please explain why there are no receipts prior to 2019. Petitioner must account from Decedent's date of death. [LRSF 14.35]. 8) On Schedule E - Please explain why parking $20.00 was disbursed when parking is absorbed as part of fees. [LRSF 14.58 B.] This must be paid back to the estate. 9) Please file receipts for the following items under Disbursements: a. 5/23/2013 Trip to San Francisco for $1,217.45 b. 06/01/2013 Flight for to arrange for the funeral $669.80 c. 01/25/2014 Asset Search Investigator fee for $1,000.00. d. 06/2013 to 02/2015 Monthly payments to Mercedes Benz $11,434.60 e. 7/17/2015 Research Potential Beneficiaries in the Philippines for $300.00. 10) On Schedule E - Please explain 5/23/2013 Trip to San Francisco-investigate decedent's death-Hotel/flight/car rental for $1,217.45. Long distance travel is reimbursed only in court's discretion, depending upon the circumstances disclosed. [LRSF 14.58 B.] 11) On Schedule E - Please explain 06/01/2013 Flight for to arrange for the funeral for $669. Long distance travel is reimbursed only in court's discretion, depending upon the circumstances disclosed. [LRSF 14.58 B.] 12) On Schedule E - Please explain 07/17/2015 Research Potential Beneficiaries in the Philippines for $300.00. Research is reimbursed only in court's discretion, depending upon the circumstances disclosed. [LRSF 14.58 B.] 13) The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. [LRSF 14.35 D.] 14) Please file a copy of the foreclosure seller's statement for the real property. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/5/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANTHONY VIDAK
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/3/21. (No earlier date available.) The second amended petition caption does not disclose all relief sought. [CRC 7.102.] Original bank letters were mailed to the Court instead of filed. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and 14.60 D.] Counsel should provide the assigned Examiner a SASE in which to return the original letters to be filed, accompanied by the required declaration by the attorney or fiduciary who will retain the originals. (Schedule C-1 was attached to the supplement filed on 3/10/21, but copies of the bank letters were attached, not the originals.) If the statutory fee/commission is based on any amount other than the I&A, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed, including the complete description of each individual security (number of shares). [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.36 I.6.] The property on hand (Schedule C) is missing the Chase account xxx5353. How can the Court attribute to the estate a portion of the funds the executor has already commingled with the trust account xxx3995 without any calculation therefor? (See paragraphs 30.d. and 34. of the second amended petition.) And all of those funds in excess of $1,336,000 are held in one savings account, which means they are not fully FDIC insured. (An amended petition completely restates and supersedes the pleading it amends for all purposes. [CRC 7.3(3). There is no need to refer to Errata to the prior pleading.) Also: personal property is movable property, not real property. (See paragraph 16.) The real property located at 1336, not 1136, Chestnut was ordered on 5/17/18 to be distributed. (See paragraph 22.h.(1).) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 4/22/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LYNN MCKANNAY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/19/2021 again for the same issues. 1) An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53]. 2) The Amended petition alleges on page 2 under #3. e.(3) all heirs at at law are adults and have waived bond. There are no waiver of bonds attached to the petition. "Amended pleading" means a pleading that completely restates and supersedes the pleading it amends for all purposes. [CRC 7.3] 3) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. (This form cannot be e-filed). [LRSF 14.18].

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JULIE HALL PINCHIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/3/21. (No earlier date available.) Request for Special Notice was filed by the Franchise Tax Board on 1/21/20. The register of actions does not reflect either an allowance or rejection of the creditor's claim. Who is the successor-in-interest to the deceased administrator? Has a personal representative been appointed in New Hampshire, or did she have a trust, or will her heirs-at-law take pursuant to Probate Code Section 13100? (Paragraph 7 of this report does not identify who the three individuals are who appear to be her relatives.) The estate has been open longer than the statutory period allowed for administering an estate. If no one petitions promptly to be appointed successor administrator, the Court may appoint the Public Administrator. Additionally, this Court does not permit accounting periods to exceed two years for a first accounting or three years for subsequent accountings. [LRSF 14.35 A.] If accounting is not waived, the first account is already delinquent and must be filed promptly. (Even if tax on the decedent's final income tax return is minor, interest and penalties could be accruing since she died almost five years ago.) A supplement to the petition must be filed, identifying the successor(s)-in-interest to the deceased administrator. A courtesy copy of the supplement and of the proof of service of notice to the Franchise Tax Board must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 4/22/21. Appearance may be required on 5/3/21 if no petition has been filed before that date to appoint a successor administrator.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANITA SILVERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/10/21. (No earlier date available.) If the statutory fee is based on any amount other than the I&A, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] This Court requires detailed time sheets to support a request for fees for extraordinary services. [CRC 7.702. The report required when an account has been waived must list the information required by law. [CRC 7.550(b).] A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the legal description of real property, the complete description of each individual security, and the (net) amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to the beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.36 I.6.] Probate Code Section 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. There is no Exhibit A attached to the petition. No courtesy copy of papers filed on 3/4/21 have been received yet (as of 3/17/21) by the Examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] The attorney fee declaration does not address how Dialyz Morales-Guerreo qualifies to bill as a paralegal. Attorney costs must also be itemized, showing the date, payee, description, and amount of each cost incurred, not lump sums. (There were personal property taxes paid by the estate?) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 4/29/21. After all of these issues are addressed, an appearance will be required for the request for extraordinary fees. Many of the services provided by the attorney appear to be ordinary or within the scope of duties of the personal representative, such as serving a Notice of Proposed Action, reviewing documents in the decedent's home, and collecting unclaimed property. It is unclear what was ambiguous about the will that it required 78+ hours to interpret it. Despite the firm's expertise, it took the equivalent of over eight weeks of full-time work to prepare the 706 and 709 and over 20 days to prepare the fee declaration. [CRC 7.703.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CARLINA ZEULI-ESPINOZA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES: 1) A Final Inventory and Appraisal has not been filed, pursuant to Probate Code 8800. 2) Neither a petition for final distribution nor the required petition for approval of status report has been filed, pursuant to Probate Code 12200.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MIKHAIL LOPYREV
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required for the court to determine whether to let this petition remain on calendar. it will not be heard on its substance as the petition has not yet been amnended to cure the issues identified by the examiner in an email to the attorney on 12/14/20. Please note, it is the duty of the petitioner to make a claim as to the characterization of the assets as CP or SP, and to provide support for that claim PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review the Court's 9th Amended General Order which took effect on 02/10/2021 for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Setting Aside Probate Homestead; Petition For Family Allowance

The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review the Court's 9th Amended General Order which took effect on 02/10/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CAROLYN GARCIA FERNANDEZ REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust'S Title To Real Property
rulings.Rulings: continued to 4/26/21 for new notice. notice of the 1st amendment was mailed 2/19/21, but the amendment was not filed until 2/22/21. notice cannot be mailed before the pleading that requires notice is filed (LRSF 14.14.A). in addition, the petitioner was asked to state exactly what day to day activities are conducted by the trustee in san francisco, and the amendment only says there are rental properties here and 90% of the work takes place here. that statement is not responsive to the examiner's inquiry as to the specific day to day activities of the trustee. a supplement must be filed with the requested information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SONIA GUTIERREZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/3/21. (No earlier date available.) No proof of service of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate has been filed yet. Item 2b of the petition is incomplete (name of the proposed administrator). Item 3d of the petition appears to be incorrect, as the annual gross income from real property is probably not $700,000; if that is the estimated value of the real property, that amount should be in item 3d(4). If the petitioner (and proposed personal representative) is the daughter, item 3g(2)(c) of the petition should have been completed. (It appears that the other heir also nominated her, in which case 3g(2(b) should also have been checked.) Item 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered, but item 7 of the petition should not be completed if issue survived. The petitioner (the person named in item 2 of the petition) is missing from item 8 of the petition. Item 2 of the Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities is incomplete (no phone number). The issues with the petition must be addressed in a supplement to the petition, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the proposed personal representative. A courtesy copy of the supplement, the proof of service of notice, and the correct (complete) Duties and Liabilities must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 4/22/21.
